title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary:
The reviewer wants two changes to the CI workflow:

1. **Add SHA-based tags to base images**: `python-base` and `venv-builder` currently only push `:latest`, which gets overwritten every run with no way to trace or roll back. Add a `:<commit-sha>` tag alongside `:latest` for immutable references.

2. **Set `fail-fast: false` on matrix jobs**: Both `build-service-images` and `smoke-test` use the default `fail-fast: true`, which will cancel sibling matrix legs on failure once more services are added. Set `fail-fast: false` so all matrix combinations run independently.

code_comments.body:
Base images tagged only as `:latest` with no immutable history.

Both `python-base` and `venv-builder` are pushed with a single `:latest` tag (lines 53 and 66). Each workflow run overwrites the previous tag, including on PR builds. While the `build-service-images` job pins to exact digests — preserving per-run reproducibility — there is no way to identify which commit produced the currently-published `:latest`, or to retrieve an earlier base image version without a digest from a workflow run artifact.

Adding a SHA-tagged label (e.g., `python-base:${{ github.sha }}`) alongside `:latest` would provide an immutable reference for auditing and rollback:

```suggestion
      - name: Build and push python-base
        id: build-python-base
        uses: docker/build-push-action@10e90e3645eae34f1e60eeb005ba3a3d33f178e8 # v6
        with:
          context: images/python-base
          platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64
          push: true
          tags: |
            ghcr.io/${{ steps.meta.outputs.owner }}/python-base:latest
            ghcr.io/${{ steps.meta.outputs.owner }}/python-base:${{ github.sha }}
          cache-from: type=gha,scope=python-base
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max,scope=python-base

      - name: Build and push venv-builder
        id: build-venv-builder
        uses: docker/build-push-action@10e90e3645eae34f1e60eeb005ba3a3d33f178e8 # v6
        with:
          context: images/venv-builder
          build-contexts: |
            python-base=docker-image://${{ steps.meta.outputs.owner }}/python-base@${{ steps.build-python-base.outputs.digest }}
          platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64
          push: true
          tags: |
            ghcr.io/${{ steps.meta.outputs.owner }}/venv-builder:latest
            ghcr.io/${{ steps.meta.outputs.owner }}/venv-builder:${{ github.sha }}
          cache-from: type=gha,scope=venv-builder
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max,scope=venv-builder
```

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Added `${{ github.sha }}` tag alongside `:latest` for both `python-base` (line 65) and `venv-builder` (line 80), matching the reviewer's suggested code exactly. Both base images now have an immutable SHA-tagged reference for auditing and rollback.

code_comments.body:
`fail-fast: true` (default) will silence failures when matrix is extended.

Both `build-service-images` (lines 77–80) and `smoke-test` (lines 189–192) use the default `fail-fast: true`. When the matrix is expanded beyond the current single `[keystone]` entry (as documented under "Adding a New Service" in `docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md`), a failure in one service/release combination will immediately cancel all remaining matrix legs. This makes it impossible to detect whether multiple services are broken in a single run; each must be fixed and re-run sequentially to reveal the next failure.

Both matrix jobs should opt out before matrix expansion occurs:

```suggestion
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        service: [keystone]
        release: ["2025.2"]
```

The same applies to the `smoke-test` job matrix (lines 189–192).

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Added `fail-fast: false` to both the `build-service-images` strategy (line 92) and the `smoke-test` strategy (line 202), matching the reviewer's suggestion. This ensures all matrix legs run to completion even when one fails, enabling detection of multiple broken services in a single run.

resolution_summary: Both review comments were addressed exactly as suggested. SHA-tagged labels were added alongside :latest for python-base and venv-builder base images, and fail-fast: false was added to both the build-service-images and smoke-test matrix strategies. No additional changes were made beyond the two files/locations mentioned in the review.
